headline: What are the risks of the Ebola virus spreading beyond the DRC?
description: Estimating the true scale of the outbreak, as well as the risk of the virus spreading both within the region and beyond, has challenged researchers, who face an epidemic with many unknowns.

headline: Coordinator of 2015 Paris attacks could be released from prison early under Belgian law
Trump's intelligence chief Tulsi Gabbard resigns
Senegalese PM Sonko claims Western 'tyranny' aims to 'impose' homosexuality on the world
description: Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko delivered a speech to lawmakers that referred to a new law, which was passed in early March and doubled prison sentences for same-sex relations, which are now punishable by five to 10 years in prison.

headline: Ex-PM Attal is officially running for president and wants to distance himself from Macron
description: Gabriel Attal announced his presidential bid on Friday. He's hoping to quickly build momentum in order to overtake Edouard Philippe, another former prime minister who is tipped as the favorite to represent the centrist bloc and the right.

headline: What we know about the Paris brawl involving Nice supporters ahead of French Cup final
description: A major brawl broke out in Paris's 10th arrondissement, where around 100 supporters of OGC Nice, who are set to face RC Lens in the French Cup final, were gathered on Thursday evening. Sixty-five people were arrested.

headline: The start-ups dreaming of creating genetically modified babies
description: Born out of the American tech sector, Preventive and Origin Genomics aim to advance the modification of embryo genomes. This highly controversial and largely banned technique has raised fears of a resurgence of a form of eugenics.

Israel has damaged or destroyed 45% of towns in southern Lebanon
description: According to an exclusive analysis of radar satellite images conducted by Le Monde and Masae Analytics, nearly half of the urban centers in southern Lebanon have been affected or even destroyed since the beginning of Israel's ground offensive in March 2026.

headline: 'Team Musul': The French youths convicted of attempting to join IS in Mozambique
description: Six young French nationals were convicted in Paris for planning to migrate to Mozambique together and join the IS-affiliated jihadist group al-Shabab in Cabo Delgado province, where it has been active since 2017.

headline: From Italy to Libya, the dehumanizing processing of migrants
description: 'Migrants in the Mediterranean: The mechanics of silence' (4/4). Exploited, extorted and abused in Libya, exiles who survive the crossing are rendered invisible and sometimes fall into the hands of mafias linked to agricultural businesses. But 'what matters is that irregular arrivals decrease,' as an official in Rome put it.

headline: European passivity in the face of Israel's far right

'In a country as polarized as Spain, it is Pedro Sánchez's international stance that garners the most support'
